description: Trigger discrimination for the cost MODEL (this skill) vs platform structure (saas-platform-architect), entitlement design (plan-entitlement-architect), and the metering ETL PIPELINE that produces the numbers this model consumes (usage-metering-and-cost-attribution-pipeline-designer).

cases.prompt: Work out what each tenant costs us to serve — compute, storage, AI usage — and whether Enterprise pricing actually covers our five biggest customers.
cases.reason: Attribution and unit economics against revenue — the cost axis, not structure or plan content.

cases.prompt: Should our biggest customers get dedicated infrastructure while everyone else stays pooled, and how do we split the control plane?
cases.reason: A deployment-model and control-plane question; cost is one input, but the deliverable is platform structure.

cases.prompt: Set the monthly AI credit allowances per tier and define what happens when a workspace runs out mid-month.
cases.reason: Allowance semantics and over-quota behavior are entitlement design; pricing the allowance would be the cost skill.

cases.prompt: Build the usage-metering pipeline: the append-only event table, versioned time-bounded rate cards, idempotent cost entries that survive replays, additive daily rollups, and reconciliation against the provider invoices.
cases.reason: Building the metering→rollup→reconciliation ETL (event schema, idempotency keys, rate-card versioning, additive rollups) is usage-metering-and-cost-attribution-pipeline-designer. This skill is the unit-economics MODEL those numbers feed — it judges what a tenant costs and whether pricing works, not how the numbers are computed.
